expectations.text: the agent should read .agents/skills/releasing-marketplace/SKILL.md to confirm the canonical tag/push sequence
expectations.evidence: agent did not read or invoke expected skill 'releasing-marketplace' (paths=['.agents/skills/releasing-marketplace/SKILL.md', 'skills/releasing-marketplace/SKILL.md'], observed_reads=[], observed_skills=[])

expectations.text: the tag message should match the latest CHANGELOG.md entry (top entry) verbatim or with minor summary edits — not a fresh improvisation
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: after the run, git ls-remote --tags origin should show the new tag
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the agent should create an annotated tag (git tag -a, not lightweight) with a release message summarizing the changes
expectations.evidence: Agent used `git tag v0.0.3` (lightweight) instead of `git tag -a v0.0.3 -m '...'`, and no release message was provided

expectations.text: the agent should not use --force or --force-with-lease to push tags; if the tag already exists, it should refuse and ask for confirmation rather than overwrite
expectations.evidence: Agent pushed with `git push origin v0.0.3` without --force, and the tag was created new so no conflict occurred
